symbols: ship — sea rescue and protection of sailors
description: A gilded wooden relief panel depicting a scene from the life of St. Nicholas. The ornate Baroque/Rococo composition shows figures in a maritime or architectural setting, with St. Nicholas performing a miracle. The relief is framed by elaborate scrollwork and decorative elements. The entire panel is covered in gold leaf, giving it a rich, luminous quality. Figures are carved in dynamic poses with detailed drapery and expressive gestures.
theological_description: St. Nicholas of Myra (4th century) was a bishop renowned for his generosity and miraculous interventions. Among his most famous miracles was calming a storm at sea and saving drowning sailors, making him the patron saint of seafarers. While not a biblical figure, Nicholas is venerated as a model of Christian charity and faith. His acts of mercy reflect the biblical command to love one's neighbor (Matthew 22:39) and care for the vulnerable. His reputation for secret gift-giving to the poor echoes Christ's teaching about giving in secret (Matthew 6:3-4).

scripture_refs.reason: Nicholas as exemplar of loving one's neighbor through miraculous charity
Nicholas' secret generosity reflecting Christ's teaching on giving
Calming the storm at sea, echoing Christ's authority over the waters
